q: What is the SI unit of force?
Which of Newton's laws explains why a seatbelt is needed in a crash?
A car accelerates from 0 to 20 m/s in 5 seconds. What is its acceleration?
Which type of energy is stored in a stretched elastic band?
What happens to the total energy in a closed system?
Why does a heavier object NOT fall faster than a lighter object in a vacuum?
o: Because air resistance cancels out mass differences
Because gravitational acceleration is the same for all masses
Because heavier objects have less inertia
Because mass affects speed but not acceleration

q: Which best describes how sound waves travel?
o: As transverse waves through a vacuum
As longitudinal waves through a medium
As electromagnetic waves through any material
As standing waves only in solids

q: What is the relationship between voltage, current and resistance (Ohm's Law)?
In a series circuit, what happens to the current at different points?
o: It stays the same throughout the circuit
It depends on the resistance of each component

q: Which best explains why a metal spoon feels colder than a wooden spoon at the same temperature?
o: Metal is naturally colder than wood
Metal conducts heat away from your hand faster than wood
Wood absorbs heat from the air
Metal has a lower temperature than wood

q: What type of electromagnetic wave has the shortest wavelength?
How does increasing the frequency of a wave affect its energy?
o: Energy only depends on wavelength, not frequency

q: Which statement correctly describes potential and kinetic energy of a pendulum at its highest point?
o: Maximum kinetic energy, minimum potential energy
Maximum potential energy, minimum kinetic energy

q: What is the main difference between mass and weight?
o: Mass and weight are the same thing
Mass is a force, weight is measured in kg
Mass measures matter, weight is the force of gravity on that matter
Weight is constant everywhere in the universe

q: Why do objects in orbit appear weightless?
o: Because there is no gravity in space
Because they are continuously falling around the Earth
Because gravity switches off at high altitude
Because they are moving too fast for gravity to act

q: Which best describes the process of refraction?
o: Light bending as it passes between different media
Light splitting into a spectrum of colours
Light being absorbed by a dark object

q: A wave has a wavelength of 2m and a frequency of 5Hz. What is its speed?
Which of the following is an example of a non-renewable energy resource?
How does insulation reduce heat loss from a house?
o: By reflecting all light away from the house
By trapping air, which is a poor conductor of heat
By generating heat within the walls
By increasing air circulation through the walls

q: What does the term 'terminal velocity' describe?
o: The maximum speed an object can theoretically reach
The speed at which air resistance equals gravitational force, so acceleration stops
The initial speed of a falling object
The speed of an object at the moment it lands
